content.title: The Flicker
content.subtitle: Origin: Multiple Dimensions · The Fast and the Fragile

entries.label: Origin
entries.value: Multiple dimensions — at least 14 documented origin points

entries.label: Appearance
entries.value: Small, slight, under 150cm. Too-long fingers. Enormous reflective eyes. Move in quick bursts.

entries.label: Build
entries.value: Smallest sapient species — light, fragile, blindingly fast

content.label: Playable Adjustments
items: +1 Reflex (Initiative only)
+1 fine dexterity (medical, repair, lockpicking)
-2 Carry capacity (small frame)
+50% food costs (burn hot, high metabolism)
1.5x human nerve speed — perceive the world in slow motion

content.label: Strengths
items: Fastest species alive — +1 Initiative, 1.5x nerve speed
Best fine motor control — +1 dexterity for precision work
Flicker medics are the best in the business — faster and steadier than augmented human hands
Dominate the Undercity informal economy — courier, medical, data processing
Flicker Guilds — most commercially successful non-human political structure
Distributed and resilient — can't pin down what you can't catch

content.label: Weaknesses
items: -2 Carry — smallest species, can't carry heavy equipment or weapons
+50% food costs — metabolism burns hot, eat twice as much
Fragile — burn out fast, lower HP ceiling
No homeland — came from so many dimensions that "home" is meaningless
Institutional distrust — history taught them that institutions classify, deploy, and suppress

content.text: Small (under 150cm), slight, enormous reflective eyes. Too-long fingers evolved for rapid manipulation. 1.5x human nerve speed — they perceive the world in slow motion. Everything else moves like it's underwater. Burn hot — high metabolism requires twice the caloric intake. Wear out fast. Move in quick bursts that other species can barely track. Not criminal by nature — entrepreneurial in environments where legal entrepreneurship isn't available.

Arrived from at least 14 different dimensions — "home dimension" is meaningless for most. Reign of Rat acquired Flickers for archival work — their speed made them efficient at cataloging. The Night of Fast Hands (2635) — The Third Uprising. Flicker networks disrupted Sentinel city-state logistics, communications, and surveillance for 72 hours. Sentinel's suppression killed 89 Flickers.

Eighty-nine names, recited at speed, in 11 seconds, simultaneously across the world, once a year. To human ears it sounds like static. To Flickers it's a memorial, political statement, and warning. The Flicker Guilds — formal economic cooperatives that have cornered markets in courier services, emergency medical, data processing, and Undercity logistics.

Mixed. Pear values them for data analysis. Reign of Rat uses them as archivists. Corporate tempo is agonizingly slow for a species that lives at 1.5x speed. Flickers don't trust institutional power. Their history taught them that institutions classify you as a resource, deploy you as a tool, and suppress you when you become inconvenient. The Undercity isn't just where they work — it's where they're safe.

Fast and fragile. Natural skirmishers and scouts. Flicker medics are the best in the business — hands faster and steadier than augmented human ones. Doc Vasik has said the best surgeon he ever saw was a Flicker working out of a margin clinic with no power and no augments. Speed makes them impossible to pin down in combat but fragility means one solid hit is devastating.

content.label: Roleplay Hooks
items: Everything moves in slow motion to you. How do you handle a world that runs at half your speed?
You recite 89 names once a year. Do you know all of them? Do you know why each one matters?
Your people don't trust institutions. How do you handle working within a crew structure?
You're the smallest person in every room. When has that been an advantage?

content.text: You can't catch what you can't see. You can't see what's already gone.
